questions.text: In a 4-4-2 formation, how many defenders does the team use?
questions.explanation: In a 4-4-2 formation the first number represents the defenders, so there are 4 defenders, 4 midfielders, and 2 forwards.

questions.text: What does the term 'pressing' mean in football tactics?
questions.explanation: Pressing refers to a defensive tactic where a team collectively moves toward the ball-carrier to win back possession as quickly as possible, reducing the opponent's time and space.

options.text: Applying pressure to win the ball back quickly as a team
A type of passing pattern played through the middle
Keeping possession while the team is ahead
A long-ball strategy aimed at the forwards

questions.text: What is the main purpose of a 'back four' in football?
questions.explanation: A back four refers to four defenders (usually two central defenders and two full-backs) whose primary role is to prevent the opposition from scoring.

options.text: To defend and prevent the opposition from scoring
To create width and provide crosses
To control possession in midfield
To score goals from set pieces

questions.text: Which position is responsible for organising the team's defensive shape and communicating with other defenders?
questions.explanation: The central defender (centre-back) is primarily responsible for organising the defensive line, communicating with teammates, and clearing danger.

options.text: Central defender (centre-back)
Goalkeeper
Defensive midfielder
Full-back

questions.text: What does it mean when a team 'plays out from the back'?
questions.explanation: Playing out from the back means building attacks by passing the ball out from the goalkeeper and defenders rather than hoofing the ball long up the field.

options.text: Building attacks through short passes starting from the goalkeeper and defenders
Always playing long balls to the strikers
Keeping the ball in the opponent's half at all times
Clearing the ball whenever under pressure

questions.explanation: A counter-attack is a quick attacking move launched immediately after winning possession, exploiting the space left by the opposition who were attacking.

options.text: A rapid attack after winning possession while the opponent is out of position
A slow, patient build-up attack
A defensive tactic used to protect a lead
A set-piece routine at corners

questions.text: What is the role of the 'false nine' in modern football?
questions.explanation: A false nine is a striker who drops deep into midfield to receive the ball, dragging centre-backs out of position and creating space for other attackers to run into.

options.text: A striker who drops into midfield to create space for teammates
A goalkeeper numbered nine
A central midfielder who plays as an attacker
A winger who plays on the wrong side

questions.explanation: Width in attack means spreading the play across the full width of the pitch, forcing the defending team to stretch and creating gaps in the centre.

options.text: Spreading play across the pitch to stretch the defence
Keeping all attackers in a narrow central channel
Playing with three strikers in a line
Avoiding the flanks to concentrate on the centre
